todaysEvents.summary: Iranian drone strike damages Kuwait power and desalination plant on Mar 30; one Indian worker killed; Kuwait Ministry of Electricity confirms major structural damage but normal operations continue; first confirmed attack on GCC civilian critical infrastructure in the conflict (Al Jazeera, CNBC, The National). (Mar 30)
todaysEvents.delta: Kuwait power plant hit; civilian infra targeted

todaysEvents.summary: Trump says US wants to 'take the oil in Iran' and seize Kharg Island export hub; posts 'big day in Iran' as Pentagon weighs full-scale ground invasion; Trump told FT preference would be to take the oil, comparing to Venezuela operation; administration has weighed occupying or blockading Kharg (CNBC, Al Jazeera, NBC News, Axios). (Mar 30)
todaysEvents.delta: Trump mulls Kharg Island seizure; ground invasion

todaysEvents.summary: US-Israel overnight strikes on Tehran power grid cause temporary blackout, since restored per Iranian authorities; Trump claims Iran agreed to 'most of the 15 points' in peace plan and let 20 vessels through Hormuz 'out of respect'; Iran officially contradicts diplomacy claim; Brent $115.93 - Brent up over 62% since Feb 27 pre-war close, record monthly surge (Al Jazeera, NBC, oilpriceapi). (Mar 30)
todaysEvents.delta: Tehran power blackout; Trump peace claim disputed

scenarios.label: Base
scenarios.description: Trump's Kharg Island rhetoric and diplomacy claims catalyse a back-channel breakthrough via Pakistan: toll booth suspended, Iran accepts partial sovereignty language, Trump refrains from power plant and Kharg strikes, Houthis pause Yemen-Israel operations. A 14-day ceasefire window opens. Brent retreats to $90-100/bbl; JKM pulls back to $17-19/MMBtu on resumed Qatar damage assessment. P&I cover only partially restored - toll booth precedent means full commercial Hormuz passage requires a separate legal framework.

scenarios.label: Stress
scenarios.description: Tehran Toll Booth ($2M/vessel in yuan) becomes the new normal: some tankers pay to transit, others reroute via Cape. Kharg Island invasion talk pressures Iran but no seizure executed. Houthis sustain Yemen-Israel strikes as a coordinated second front. Iran attacks continue on GCC civilian infrastructure (Kuwait precedent). Trump extends April 6 deadline again. Brent settles $110-130/bbl; JKM at $19-23/MMBtu. Iran parliament legislates the toll. iTraxx Asia IG pushes through 185bp.

scenarios.label: Tail
scenarios.description: US executes Kharg Island seizure or Trump resumes power plant strikes after April 6; Iran revokes non-hostile framework and closes toll corridor; full commercial blockade reinstated. Houthis escalate to daily Israel strikes; ground invasion preparations confirmed. IRGC resumes full offensive operations against US/GCC targets; Kuwait and Saudi civilian infrastructure under sustained attack. Ras Laffan offline for the quarter. Oil hits $160-190/bbl; LNG spot markets seize. ASEAN recession risk surges.
